Document:
# Surgery Surgery is hands-on medical work: stitching up injuries and pulling out (or putting in) organs. You'll need a downed patient, a bedsheet, and the right surgical tools.
## Requirements To begin surgery, you need: - A [color=#a4885c]downed patient[/color] (you cannot operate on standing or conscious patients) - A [color=#a4885c]bedsheet[/color] to drape the patient - The correct [color=#a4885c]surgical tools[/color] for each step
You [color=#993333]cannot perform surgery on yourself[/color].
## Surgical Tools
## Starting Surgery 1. Make sure your patient is [color=#a4885c]downed[/color] (in crit, stunned, etc.) 2. Use a [color=#a4885c]bedsheet[/color] on the patient to open the procedure menu 3. Select a procedure from the radial menu 4. The patient is now draped and surgery begins
The bedsheet is only consumed when you confirm a procedure. Cancelling the menu wastes nothing.
## Surgery Surfaces Buckle your patient to a surgery surface before starting, and the procedure goes faster:
Operating without a proper surface uses the default speed (1.0x).
## Performing Steps Each procedure is a sequence of tool steps. Use the [color=#a4885c]correct tool[/color] on the draped patient to advance. Using the wrong tool gives a warning popup.
When all steps are done, use a [color=#a4885c]cautery[/color] on the patient to close the incision and finish the procedure. The cautery stops all bleeding and removes the drape.
## Procedures
### Tend Wounds (Brute) Repairs physical damage (blunt, slash, piercing). - [color=#a4885c]Scalpel[/color] - make incision - [color=#a4885c]Retractor[/color] - retract skin - [color=#a4885c]Hemostat[/color] - treat wounds (repeats automatically until healed) - [color=#a4885c]Cautery[/color] - close incision
### Tend Wounds (Burn) Treats burn damage (heat, shock, cold, caustic). - [color=#a4885c]Scalpel[/color] - make incision - [color=#a4885c]Retractor[/color] - retract skin - [color=#a4885c]Hemostat[/color] - treat burns (repeats automatically until healed) - [color=#a4885c]Cautery[/color] - close incision
### Organ Manipulation Open the body cavity to remove or insert organs. - [color=#a4885c]Scalpel[/color] - make incision - [color=#a4885c]Retractor[/color] - retract skin - [color=#a4885c]Hemostat[/color] - clamp vessels - [color=#a4885c]Bone saw[/color] - saw through bone - [color=#a4885c]Hemostat[/color] - select organ to remove (opens organ picker) - [color=#a4885c]Cautery[/color] - close incision
To [color=#a4885c]insert[/color] an organ, hold it in your hand and use it on the draped patient during an active surgery.
## Cancelling Surgery The patient can click the [color=#a4885c]drape alert[/color] on their HUD to remove the drape and cancel the procedure at any time.
## Tips - Surgery causes some damage during the opening steps (incisions bleed, retractors bruise). Plan for this. - The hemostat heals a fixed total per repeat, split across whatever damage types the patient actually has. - Keep extra bedsheets handy. Each procedure uses one. - Medbay's operating table is the fastest surface available.
